canonical_question: How do Kubernetes Network Policies work and how do I implement them?
aliases: How do I restrict pod-to-pod traffic in Kubernetes?
What is a Kubernetes NetworkPolicy and how do I write one?
How do I create a default deny network policy in Kubernetes?
How do I control ingress and egress traffic between Kubernetes pods?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: v1 GA in Kubernetes 1.7 (2017); no breaking changes since

constraints: NetworkPolicy has NO effect without a CNI plugin that implements enforcement (Calico, Cilium, Weave, Antrea, Kube-router)
Flannel does NOT support NetworkPolicy natively; requires a secondary CNI
Policies are namespace-scoped; you must create policies in each namespace separately
Policies are additive (union); you cannot deny a specific flow allowed by another policy
Traffic to/from the pod's node is ALWAYS allowed regardless of NetworkPolicy rules

skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Cilium Network Policies or a service mesh (Istio/Linkerd)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: You need cluster-wide policies across all namespaces
You need to control external HTTP routing to services

inputs_needed.question: Which CNI plugin is installed in the cluster?
What traffic direction do you want to control?
